  </div>
  <div class="nav-item" onclick="showPage('audit')">
    <svg width="15" height="15" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.8" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path d="M14 2H6a2 2 0 0 0-2 2v16a2 2 0 0 0 2 2h12a2 2 0 0 0 2-2V8z"/><polyline points="14 2 14 8 20 8"/><line x1="16" y1="13" x2="8" y2="13"/><line x1="16" y1="17" x2="8" y2="17"/><polyline points="10 9 9 9 8 9"/></svg>
    Audit Log
  </div>
</aside>

<!-- main -->
<main class="main">

  <!-- ── Listeners page ── -->
  <div id="page-listeners" class="page active">
    <div class="page-header">
      <div class="page-title">ALB / NLB TLS Listeners</div>
      <div class="page-sub">Read-only discovery. Red rows need migration. Click "Open migration PR" to propose the change.</div>
    </div>

    <div class="toolbar">
      <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:4px;">
        <label style="font-family:var(--mono);font-size:10px;color:var(--muted);text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;">AWS Region</label>
        <input type="text" id="region-input" value="us-east-1" placeholder="us-east-1">
      </div>
      <div style="align-self:flex-end">
        <button class="btn btn-primary" onclick="loadListeners()">
          <span id="discover-spinner" style="display:none" class="spinner"></span>
          Discover Listeners
        </button>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="stat-grid" id="stats" style="display:none">
      <div class="stat-card"><div class="stat-label">Total Listeners</div><div class="stat-value" id="stat-total">0</div></div>
      <div class="stat-card"><div class="stat-label">Needs Migration</div><div class="stat-value" style="color:var(--critical)" id="stat-vuln">0</div></div>
      <div class="stat-card"><div class="stat-label">PQ Ready</div><div class="stat-value" style="color:var(--low)" id="stat-pq">0</div></div>
    </div>

    <div class="table-wrap">
      <table id="listener-table" style="display:none">
        <thead>
          <tr>
            <th>Load Balancer</th>
            <th>Port / Proto</th>
            <th>Policy</th>
            <th>Environment</th>
            <th>PQ Status</th>
            <th>Actions</th>
          </tr>
        </thead>
        <tbody id="listener-tbody"></tbody>
      </table>
    </div>
    <div id="listener-empty" class="empty-state" style="display:none">
      <div class="icon">🔍</div>
      <div>No HTTPS/TLS listeners found in this region.</div>
    </div>
    <div id="listener-error" class="notice notice-warn" style="display:none"></div>
  </div>

  <!-- ── Audit log page ── -->
  <div id="page-audit" class="page">
    <div class="page-header">
      <div class="page-title">Audit Log</div>
      <div class="page-sub">Append-only record of every Cryptiq action. Never modified.</div>
    </div>
    <div class="toolbar">
      <button class="btn btn-ghost" onclick="loadAudit()">Refresh</button>
    </div>
    <div id="audit-list"></div>
    <div id="audit-empty" class="empty-state" style="display:none">
      <div class="icon">📋</div>
      <div>No audit entries yet. Run a scan or migration.</div>
    </div>
  </div>

</main>
</div>

<!-- ── Migration modal ── -->
<div class="modal-backdrop" id="migrate-modal">
  <div class="modal">
    <div class="modal-header">
      <div class="modal-title" id="modal-title">Migration Preview</div>
      <button class="modal-close" onclick="closeModal('migrate-modal')">×</button>
    </div>
    <div class="modal-body">
      <div class="notice notice-info" style="margin-bottom:14px">
        This is a <strong>dry run</strong>. Review the diff below, then click "Open PR" to propose the change.
        Cryptiq will not merge the PR — a human does that.
      </div>

      <div style="margin-bottom:14px">
        <div style="font-family:var(--mono);font-size:11px;color:var(--muted);text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;margin-bottom:6px">GitHub Repo (owner/name)</div>
        <input type="text" id="modal-gh-repo" placeholder="e.g. acmecorp/infra" style="width:100%">
      </div>
      <div style="margin-bottom:14px">
        <div style="font-family:var(--mono);font-size:11px;color:var(--muted);text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;margin-bottom:6px">Terraform Repo Path (local)</div>
        <input type="text" id="modal-tf-repo" placeholder="e.g. /home/user/infra" style="width:100%">
      </div>

      <div style="font-family:var(--mono);font-size:11px;color:var(--muted);text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;margin-bottom:8px">Proposed Diff</div>
      <pre class="diff" id="modal-diff">Click "Preview Diff" to load.</pre>

      <div id="modal-pr-result" style="display:none;margin-top:14px">
        <div class="notice notice-info">PR opened: <a id="modal-pr-link" href="#" target="_blank" style="color:var(--accent)"></a></div>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="modal-footer">
      <button class="btn btn-ghost" onclick="closeModal('migrate-modal')">Cancel</button>
      <button class="btn btn-ghost" id="modal-preview-btn" onclick="previewDiff()">
        <span id="preview-spinner" style="display:none" class="spinner"></span>
        Preview Diff
      </button>
      <button class="btn btn-primary" id="modal-open-pr-btn" onclick="openPR()" disabled>
        <span id="open-pr-spinner" style="display:none" class="spinner"></span>
        Open PR
      </button>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<script>
let _listeners = [];
let _currentListener = null;
let _lastDryRunResult = null;

// ── Navigation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
function showPage(name) {
  document.querySelectorAll('.page').forEach(p => p.classList.remove('active'));
  document.querySelectorAll('.nav-item').forEach(n => n.classList.remove('active'));
  document.getElementById('page-' + name).classList.add('active');
  event.currentTarget.classList.add('active');
  if (name === 'audit') loadAudit();
}

// ── Discovery ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
async function loadListeners() {
  const region = document.getElementById('region-input').value.trim() || 'us-east-1';
  const spinner = document.getElementById('discover-spinner');
  const errEl = document.getElementById('listener-error');
  const statsEl = document.getElementById('stats');
  const tableEl = document.getElementById('listener-table');
  const emptyEl = document.getElementById('listener-empty');

  spinner.style.display = 'inline-block';
  errEl.style.display = 'none';
  statsEl.style.display = 'none';
  tableEl.style.display = 'none';
  emptyEl.style.display = 'none';

  try {
    const res = await fetch(`/aws/alb-listeners?region=${encodeURIComponent(region)}`);
    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(await res.text());
    const data = await res.json();
    _listeners = data.listeners || [];
    renderListeners(_listeners);
  } catch (e) {
    errEl.textContent = 'Discovery failed: ' + e.message;
    errEl.style.display = 'block';
  } finally {
    spinner.style.display = 'none';
  }
}

function renderListeners(listeners) {
  const statsEl = document.getElementById('stats');
  const tableEl = document.getElementById('listener-table');
  const emptyEl = document.getElementById('listener-empty');
  const tbody = document.getElementById('listener-tbody');

  if (!listeners.length) {
    emptyEl.style.display = 'block';
    return;
  }

  const vuln = listeners.filter(l => !l.is_post_quantum).length;
  const pq = listeners.length - vuln;
  document.getElementById('stat-total').textContent = listeners.length;
  document.getElementById('stat-vuln').textContent = vuln;
  document.getElementById('stat-pq').textContent = pq;
  statsEl.style.display = 'grid';

  tbody.innerHTML = '';
  listeners.forEach(l => {
    const pqPill = l.is_post_quantum
      ? '<span class="pill pill-green">PQ Ready</span>'
      : '<span class="pill pill-red">Needs Migration</span>';
    const envPill = l.environment
      ? `<span class="pill ${l.environment === 'prod' ? 'pill-red' : 'pill-muted'}">${l.environment}</span>`
      : '<span class="pill pill-muted">untagged</span>';

    const actionBtn = l.is_post_quantum
      ? '<span style="color:var(--muted);font-size:12px">PQ Ready</span>'
      : `<button class="btn btn-danger btn-sm" onclick="openMigrateModal(${JSON.stringify(l).replace(/"/g, '&quot;')})">Open Migration PR</button>`;

    const tr = document.createElement('tr');
    tr.innerHTML = `
      <td><span style="font-family:var(--mono)">${l.lb_name}</span></td>
      <td><span style="font-family:var(--mono)">${l.port} / ${l.protocol}</span></td>
      <td><span style="font-family:var(--mono);font-size:11px;color:var(--muted)">${l.ssl_policy_name}</span></td>
      <td>${envPill}</td>
      <td>${pqPill}</td>
      <td>${actionBtn}</td>
    `;
    tbody.appendChild(tr);
  });

  tableEl.style.display = 'table';
}

// ── Migration modal ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
function openMigrateModal(listener) {
  _currentListener = listener;
  _lastDryRunResult = null;
  document.getElementById('modal-title').textContent = `Migrate ${listener.lb_name}:${listener.port}`;
  document.getElementById('modal-diff').textContent = 'Click "Preview Diff" to load.';
  document.getElementById('modal-pr-result').style.display = 'none';
  document.getElementById('modal-open-pr-btn').disabled = true;
  document.getElementById('migrate-modal').classList.add('open');
}

function closeModal(id) {
  document.getElementById(id).classList.remove('open');
}

async function previewDiff() {
  if (!_currentListener) return;
  const ghRepo = document.getElementById('modal-gh-repo').value.trim();
  const tfRepo = document.getElementById('modal-tf-repo').value.trim();
  if (!ghRepo || !tfRepo) { alert('Enter GitHub repo and Terraform path.'); return; }

  const spinner = document.getElementById('preview-spinner');
  spinner.style.display = 'inline-block';
  document.getElementById('modal-preview-btn').disabled = true;

  try {
    const res = await fetch('/migrate/alb-tls', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: {'Content-Type': 'application/json'},
      body: JSON.stringify({
        listener_arn: _currentListener.listener_arn,
        tf_repo: tfRepo,
        gh_repo: ghRepo,
        dry_run: true
      })
    });
    const data = await res.json();
    if (data.status === 'dry_run' && data.diff) {
      _lastDryRunResult = data;
      renderDiff(data.diff);
      document.getElementById('modal-open-pr-btn').disabled = false;
    } else {
      document.getElementById('modal-diff').textContent = JSON.stringify(data, null, 2);
    }
  } catch (e) {
    document.getElementById('modal-diff').textContent = 'Error: ' + e.message;
  } finally {
    spinner.style.display = 'none';
    document.getElementById('modal-preview-btn').disabled = false;
  }
}

function renderDiff(diff) {
  const pre = document.getElementById('modal-diff');
  pre.innerHTML = '';
  diff.split('\n').forEach(line => {
    const span = document.createElement('span');
    span.textContent = line + '\n';
    if (line.startsWith('-') && !line.startsWith('---')) span.className = 'del';
    else if (line.startsWith('+') && !line.startsWith('+++')) span.className = 'add';
    pre.appendChild(span);
  });
}

async function openPR() {
  if (!_currentListener || !_lastDryRunResult) return;
  const ghRepo = document.getElementById('modal-gh-repo').value.trim();
  const tfRepo = document.getElementById('modal-tf-repo').value.trim();
  if (!confirm('Open a real migration PR in GitHub now? Cryptiq will NOT merge it.')) return;

  const spinner = document.getElementById('open-pr-spinner');
  spinner.style.display = 'inline-block';
  document.getElementById('modal-open-pr-btn').disabled = true;

  try {
    const res = await fetch('/migrate/alb-tls', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: {'Content-Type': 'application/json'},
      body: JSON.stringify({
        listener_arn: _currentListener.listener_arn,
        tf_repo: tfRepo,
        gh_repo: ghRepo,
        dry_run: false
      })
    });
    const data = await res.json();
    if (data.pr_url) {
      const linkEl = document.getElementById('modal-pr-link');
      linkEl.href = data.pr_url;
      linkEl.textContent = data.pr_url;
      document.getElementById('modal-pr-result').style.display = 'block';
    } else {
      alert('Unexpected response: ' + JSON.stringify(data));
    }
  } catch (e) {
    alert('Error: ' + e.message);
  } finally {
    spinner.style.display = 'none';
  }
}

// ── Audit log ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
async function loadAudit() {
  const listEl = document.getElementById('audit-list');
  const emptyEl = document.getElementById('audit-empty');
  listEl.innerHTML = '<div style="color:var(--muted);font-size:13px">Loading…</div>';
  emptyEl.style.display = 'none';

  try {
    const res = await fetch('/audit-log?limit=200');
    const data = await res.json();
    const entries = (data.entries || []).reverse();
    if (!entries.length) {
      listEl.innerHTML = '';
      emptyEl.style.display = 'block';
      return;
    }
    listEl.innerHTML = entries.map(e => `
      <div class="audit-entry">
        <span class="audit-ts">${e.timestamp}</span>
        &nbsp;·&nbsp;
        <span class="audit-action">${e.action}</span>
        &nbsp;→&nbsp;
        <span class="audit-outcome-${e.outcome}">${e.outcome}</span>
        &nbsp;·&nbsp;
        <span style="color:var(--muted)">${e.target}</span>
        ${e.pr_url ? `&nbsp;<a href="${e.pr_url}" target="_blank" style="color:var(--accent)">PR ↗</a>` : ''}
      </div>
    `).join('');
  } catch (e) {
    listEl.innerHTML = `<div style="color:var(--critical)">Failed to load audit log: ${e.message}</div>`;
  }
}
</script>
</body>
</html>

```

```md
# Demo Script — ALB TLS → PQC Migration

End-to-end walkthrough from a fresh AWS account to a demoable PQC migration.
Run these steps in order. Expected output is shown at each step.

---

## Prerequisites

- AWS credentials configured (`aws configure` or `AWS_*` env vars)
- Terraform ≥ 1.5 installed
- Python env set up: `pip install -r requirements.txt && pip install -r ssh_scanner/requirements.txt`
- `GITHUB_TOKEN` env var set with `repo` scope (or `public_repo` for public repos)
- `CRYPTIQ_ACTOR` env var set (e.g. `export CRYPTIQ_ACTOR=yourname`)

---

## Step 1 — Provision the demo ALB

\```bash
cd demo-infra
terraform init
terraform plan   # Review: should create 6 resources (ALB, listener, SG, cert, TG, etc.)
terraform apply  # YOU apply this — Cryptiq never applies infrastructure changes
\```

**Expected output:**
\```
Apply complete! Resources: 6 added, 0 changed, 0 destroyed.

Outputs:
alb_arn           = "arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789:loadbalancer/app/cryptiq-pqc-demo-alb/..."
listener_arn      = "arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789:listener/app/..."
current_ssl_policy = "ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06"
\```

---

## Step 2 — Start the Cryptiq server

\```bash
cd ..   # back to repo root
python api.py
\```

**Expected output:**
\```
  Cryptiq PQC Scanner
  Home      →  http://localhost:8000
  ALB       →  http://localhost:8000/alb
  TLS       →  http://localhost:8000/tls
  SSH       →  http://localhost:8000/ssh
  Docs      →  http://localhost:8000/docs
\```

---

## Step 3 — Discover the demo ALB (API)

\```bash
curl -s "http://localhost:8000/aws/alb-listeners?region=us-east-1" | python -m json.tool
\```

**Expected output (abridged):**
\```json
{
  "region": "us-east-1",
  "count": 1,
  "listeners": [
    {
      "lb_name": "cryptiq-pqc-demo-alb",
      "port": 443,
      "protocol": "HTTPS",
      "ssl_policy_name": "ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06",
      "is_post_quantum": false,
      "environment": "staging"
    }
  ]
}
\```

`is_post_quantum: false` — this is your migration target.

---

## Step 4 — Discover via the dashboard

Open **http://localhost:8000/alb** in a browser.

1. Enter region `us-east-1` and click **Discover Listeners**
2. You should see 1 row: `cryptiq-pqc-demo-alb` with a red **Needs Migration** pill

---

## Step 5 — Preview the migration diff (dry run)

Click **Open Migration PR** on the red row. In the modal:

1. Enter your GitHub repo: e.g. `yourname/infra-demo`
2. Enter the path to `demo-infra/` on your machine: e.g. `/path/to/Cryptiq-MVP/demo-infra`
3. Click **Preview Diff**

**Expected diff (in modal):**
\```diff
-  ssl_policy        = "ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06"
+  ssl_policy        = "ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-Res-PQ-2025-09"
\```

Exactly one line changes. No other fields are touched.

You can also do this via API:
\```bash
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8000/migrate/alb-tls \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "listener_arn": "<LISTENER_ARN_FROM_STEP_2>",
    "tf_repo": "/path/to/Cryptiq-MVP/demo-infra",
    "gh_repo": "yourname/infra-demo",
    "dry_run": true
  }' | python -m json.tool
\```

---

## Step 6 — Open the real migration PR

In the modal, click **Open PR** (or set `dry_run: false` in the API call).

**Expected:**
- A PR opens in your GitHub repo
- PR title: `cryptiq: migrate cryptiq-pqc-demo-alb:443 to PQ TLS policy`
- PR body contains: current policy, target policy, diff, and: *"This tool will not merge for you"*
- PR is labeled `cryptiq-migration`

---

## Step 7 — Merge the PR (human action)

In GitHub, review and merge the PR. This is the only step Cryptiq does not do.

After merging, your CI/CD (or `terraform apply`) applies the change:
\```bash
cd demo-infra
terraform apply   # should show: 1 to change (ssl_policy only)
\```

---

## Step 8 — Verify the listener went green

Refresh the dashboard at **http://localhost:8000/alb** → Discover Listeners again.

The row should now show a green **PQ Ready** pill.

\```bash
curl -s "http://localhost:8000/aws/alb-listeners?region=us-east-1" | python -c "
import sys,json
d=json.load(sys.stdin)
for l in d['listeners']:
    print(l['lb_name'], '→', 'PQ READY' if l['is_post_quantum'] else 'NEEDS MIGRATION')
"
\```

**Expected:**
\```
cryptiq-pqc-demo-alb → PQ READY
\```

---

## Step 9 — Roll back

\```bash
# Get the PR number and body from GitHub, then:
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8000/migrate/alb-tls/rollback \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "listener_arn": "<LISTENER_ARN>",
    "migration_pr_body": "<FULL PR BODY FROM STEP 6>",
    "migration_pr_number": <PR_NUMBER>,
    "tf_file": "/path/to/Cryptiq-MVP/demo-infra/main.tf",
    "gh_repo": "yourname/infra-demo",
    "dry_run": true
  }' | python -m json.tool
\```

Review the rollback diff (should restore original policy), then set `dry_run: false` to open the rollback PR.

After merging and applying the rollback, the listener reverts to `ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06`.

---

## Step 10 — Check the audit log

\```bash
curl -s "http://localhost:8000/audit-log" | python -m json.tool
\```

Or view it on the **Audit Log** tab in the dashboard.

**Expected entries:** `discovery`, `plan`, `open_pr`, `rollback` — all timestamped and immutable.

---

## Step 11 — Teardown

\```bash
cd demo-infra
terraform destroy   # removes all 6 resources; no ongoing charges
\```

All resources are tagged `Project = cryptiq-pqc-demo` — verify in the AWS console that nothing was missed.

---

## demo-reset (return to starting state)

Run this to destroy infra, clear the audit log, and reset the DB:

\```bash
bash demo-reset.sh
\```

or on Windows:

\```powershell
.\demo-reset.ps1
\```

---

## Troubleshooting

| Symptom | Fix |
|---|---|
| `No listeners found` | Confirm terraform apply completed; check region matches |
| `manual_review_required` | The TF file has multiple `aws_lb_listener` blocks — identify the one to migrate and narrow the search |
| `GITHUB_TOKEN not set` | `export GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_...` |
| PR body missing metadata | Ensure `dry_run=true` result was used as `migration_pr_body` for rollback |
| `prod_blocked` | Pass `allow_prod=true` and `prod_token="I-UNDERSTAND-PROD"` |

```

```

```sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# demo-reset.sh — return everything to the starting state after a demo
set -e

echo "==> Destroying demo infra..."
cd demo-infra
terraform destroy -auto-approve
cd ..

echo "==> Clearing audit log..."
rm -f out/audit.log

rm -f cryptiq.db ssh_scanner/ssh_scanner.db

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-06-27 23:06:27Z)**_

## How the ALB TLS → PQC Migration Path Works

The system has one job: **find classical TLS on AWS load balancers, propose a fix as a pull request, and never touch anything without a human approving it first.**

Here's how it flows end to end.

---

### 1. Discovery (`tls_scanner/scan_alb.py`)

When you hit `GET /aws/alb-listeners`, the engine makes three read-only AWS calls in sequence:

```
describe_load_balancers  →  gets all ALBs/NLBs in the region
describe_listeners       →  for each LB, gets its HTTPS/TLS listeners
describe_ssl_policies    →  resolves what each ssl_policy name actually means
describe_tags            →  reads the "Environment" tag on each LB
```

For every listener it finds, it decides `is_post_quantum` using a simple rule: is the policy name in the known PQ set (`ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-Res-PQ-2025-09`)? If yes, green. If no, red. This check is name-based because AWS embeds the PQ signal in the policy name itself — the `# VERIFY:` comments in the code flag that these names change quarterly and need manual re-confirmation before shipping.

Each listener becomes a `TlsListenerAsset` dataclass — the typed record that everything downstream operates on.

---

### 2. The diff (`tls_migration/alb_plan.py`)

When you click "Open Migration PR" on a red row, the first thing the engine does is compute a Terraform diff **without touching AWS or GitHub at all**.

It walks the `.tf` files in your Terraform repo looking for `resource "aws_lb_listener"` blocks. It uses brace-counting (not regex alone) to find where each block starts and ends. Then it applies a very narrow substitution:

```python
ssl_policy = "ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06"
→
ssl_policy = "ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-Res-PQ-2025-09"
```

**If anything is ambiguous** — two listeners in the same file, can't match which one maps to this ARN — it returns `manual_review_required` immediately and stops. It never guesses. After the substitution it counts the changed lines in the diff and verifies exactly two lines changed (one removal, one addition). Any other count also triggers manual review.

The result is a standard unified diff string — the same format `git diff` produces — that you can read and review.

---

### 3. Safety checks before GitHub (`tls_migration/run.py`)

Before opening any PR, the orchestrator runs three gates:

**Gate 1 — Already migrated?** If `is_post_quantum=True`, exit cleanly. No duplicate PRs.

**Gate 2 — Prod environment?** If the ALB has `Environment=prod` tag, the request is blocked unless `allow_prod=True` AND the caller provides the literal string `"I-UNDERSTAND-PROD"` as a confirmation token. This is intentionally annoying.

**Gate 3 — Diff valid?** If `alb_plan.py` returned `manual_review_required`, the orchestrator surfaces that back to the caller and stops.

Only after all three gates pass does the engine proceed to GitHub.

---

### 4. The GitHub chokepoint (`tls_migration/github_pr.py`)

Every GitHub write in the entire codebase routes through one function: `open_migration_pr()`. At the top of this function, before any API call, there's a hard assert:

```python
_ALLOWED_OPERATIONS = frozenset({"branch_create", "commit", "open_pr"})

def _assert_allowed(operation: str) -> None:
    if operation not in _ALLOWED_OPERATIONS:
        raise PermissionError(...)
```

This is called before each of the three GitHub steps — create branch, commit file, open PR. If anything else (merge, force-push, delete) were ever called here, it would raise `PermissionError` and stop. The test suite verifies this: `test_chokepoint_rejects_merge` calls `assert_not_merge("merge_pull_request")` and asserts the exception fires.

The PR body includes a block that's both human-readable and machine-readable:

```
<!-- cryptiq-metadata
original_policy=ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06
listener_arn=arn:aws:...
-->
```

This metadata block is what makes rollback safe — the original policy is stored in the PR, not inferred later.

---

### 5. Dry-run vs real run

Every migration endpoint defaults to `dry_run=True`. In dry-run mode, the engine runs discovery, computes the diff, builds the full PR body, and returns all of that — but makes zero GitHub calls. You see exactly what the PR would look like before committing to it.

When you click "Open PR" in the modal (or pass `dry_run=false` to the API), the same code path runs, and the only difference is the final call to `open_migration_pr()` is not skipped.

---

### 6. Rollback (`tls_migration/rollback.py`)

Rollback works by reading the `<!-- cryptiq-metadata -->` block from the original migration PR body and extracting `original_policy`. It **never reads the current state of the Terraform file** to determine what to revert to — it uses the stored value. This means:

- The rollback is byte-identical to the original, even if someone manually edited the file in the meantime
- There's no "what was it before?" ambiguity

It then generates the inverse diff:

```
ssl_policy = "ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-Res-PQ-2025-09"  (current)
→
ssl_policy = "ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06"          (original)
```

And opens a second PR via the same chokepoint. The round-trip test (`test_migrate_then_rollback_restores_original`) applies migrate → rollback and asserts the `ssl_policy` line is byte-identical to the starting state.

---

### 7. Audit log (`tls_migration/audit.py`)

Every action — discovery, plan, dry-run, PR opened, rollback — appends one JSON line to `out/audit.log`. It's append-only: the file is opened with `"a"` mode and no line is ever removed or modified. The `/audit-log` endpoint and the Audit Log tab in the dashboard read from this file. The `CRYPTIQ_ACTOR` env var stamps who triggered each action.

---

### 8. The UI (`static/alb.html`)

The dashboard is vanilla HTML/JS that calls the same API endpoints. The flow mirrors the CLI:

1. "Discover Listeners" → `GET /aws/alb-listeners` → renders table with red/green pills
2. "Open Migration PR" on a red row → opens modal → "Preview Diff" calls `POST /migrate/alb-tls?dry_run=true` → renders the colored diff
3. "Open PR" → calls `POST /migrate/alb-tls?dry_run=false` → displays PR link
4. Audit Log tab → `GET /audit-log` → lists every action

The UI calls the exact same guardrailed endpoints as the CLI. There are no shortcut routes.

---

### What never happens

The engine holds **no AWS write credentials**. It can only read. The merged PR is applied by the customer's own CI/CD pipeline with the customer's own credentials. Cryptiq's only write surface is GitHub (create branch → commit → open PR), and even that is hard-capped at those three operations by the chokepoint. The worst a credential compromise can do is read TLS metadata and open a pull request.

---
